theme: CDD v0.1.0 — Core abstractions: Loop, Adapter, Generator, Log + reference text adapter + echo generator. Shipped as a public Python package (srbryers/cdd, MIT) with editable install dogfooded from FATHOMS. 3-round Deep tier review (code + architect, parallel) returned APPROVE-WITH-CHANGES R1, APPROVE R2, APPROVE R3. 16 R1 code findings + 16 R1 architect findings triaged: 16 fixed in this PR, 12 deferred (5 to new v0.1.5 'Protocol hardening' sprint inserted between v0.1.0 and v0.2.0; 3 to v0.2.0; 4 to later sprints), 4 accepted. 1 R2 architect note (docstring tightening) folded. 116 tests pass. Real recovery incident at merge time: chained `gh pr merge && tag && branch -d` swallowed the merge failure, tagged the wrong commit, and deleted the feature branch. Recovered via refs/pull/1/head fetch + bad-tag-delete + FF merge + re-tag. ~10min cost. Saved feedback memory on the chained-destructive-ops failure mode.

tickets.title: Define Loop, LogEntry, Verdict — core types
hazards: Verdict + DeterminismTier as StrEnum (round-trips through YAML cleanly).
ModelIdentity + LogEntry as frozen dataclasses (immutability for authorship trail).
LogEntry.extra_params: dict[str, Any] — frozen dataclass freezes the binding, not the dict contents. Documented as runtime convention, not enforced.
Architect F11 deferred: single-int seed assumption on LogEntry. v0.2.0 ticket S4-3.
tickets.notes: Type contract is the persistence contract. 18 tests pin the shape.

tickets.title: Define Adapter protocol (artifact-type interface)
hazards: @runtime_checkable Protocol (not ABC). Adapters duck-type; no inheritance forced. isinstance(obj, Adapter) works at runtime.
Type contract on raw_output is documented runtime convention (TypeError on mismatch), not generic-typed. Defer Adapter[T] generics to v0.1.5/S3-1.
Architect F1 deferred: generate(prompt: str) -> Any does not survive multi-modal/streaming/async. v0.1.5 ticket S3-1.
Architect F13 fix-folded: docstring documents snake_case-for-artifact_type vs PascalCase-for-class naming convention.
tickets.notes: Protocol is the cleavage CDD needed. Adapter owns artifact type, Generator owns provider, Loop wires them.

tickets.title: Define Generator protocol (AI-provider interface)
hazards: model_identity property + supports_seed property + generate(prompt, seed, extra_params) -> Any.
Architect F5 deferred: determinism_tier on Adapter when Generator decides reproducibility. v0.1.5 ticket S3-3 makes Loop negotiate tier from adapter+generator+user request.
Architect F14 deferred: supports_seed naming is misleading on EchoGenerator (deterministic but not RNG-seeded). v0.7.0+ ticket S9-2 (rename in API freeze sprint).
EchoGenerator reference impl ships with declarative supports_seed=True for tests.
tickets.notes: Smallest possible protocol. Real generators (Anthropic, OpenAI, fal.ai) land in v0.2.0/S4.

tickets.title: Log schema v1 — versioned YAML, replay-capable
hazards: Architect F2 fix-folded: schema version is now SemVer 'MAJOR.MINOR' (was int 1). Same-MAJOR forward-compat: higher MINOR reads with warnings.warn(stacklevel=3); lower MINOR reads cleanly; different MAJOR raises LogSchemaError.
Architect F6 fix-folded: _LOG_SCHEMA_VERSION underscore-private; not in __all__. Public API drops to 16 symbols.
Architect F12 fix-folded: Log.parent_session_id added (str | None = None) — multi-session authorship lineage hook. Round-trips; omitted from YAML when None.
Code F1 + F2 + F7 fix-folded: from_dict guards on non-dict / missing version key / replays each entry through Log.append for chain integrity on read.
Code F8 fix-folded: 6 negative tests added (empty file, null YAML, scalar YAML, missing version, corrupt parent_id, iteration skip).
Code F11 accepted: to_dict/from_dict not exported from __init__ (kept private alongside _LOG_SCHEMA_VERSION).
Code F15 accepted: Log.entries is a list — direct mutation bypasses append discipline. Documented as 'append is the discipline.'
Architect F8 deferred: YAML scaling cliff for 100+ iteration logs. v0.4.0 ticket S6-3 (JSONL append mode).
Architect F10 deferred: extra_params has no schema. v0.6.0 ticket S8-4 (Adapter.params_schema).
tickets.notes: 19 base tests + 13 fix-fold tests = 32 tests on the log layer. Schema version + chain replay are the most load-bearing decisions.

tickets.title: Reference text adapter + EchoGenerator + Loop integration
hazards: TextFileAdapter content-addressed via SHA-256 prefix (16 hex chars = 64 bits per-namespace).
Code F3 fix-folded: path-traversal defense in persist + load (rejects ../ traversal, absolute-path injection, namespace with separators or leading dot, empty namespace).
Code F12 fix-folded: HASH_PREFIX_LEN docstring tightened to articulate per-namespace collision domain.
Code F14 fix-folded: empty-string round-trip test added.
EchoGenerator deterministic stub for tests-without-API-keys.
Loop holds at most one pending proposal at a time. propose() then review() forces the discipline at the type level.
Code F9 fix-folded: Loop(session_id=X, log=Y_with_different_id) raises LoopStateError instead of silently ignoring session_id.
Architect F3 + F9 deferred: synchronous eager persist forces 4096x4096 mask writes per iteration for vegetation adapter. v0.1.5 ticket S3-2 (stage/commit/discard).

Architect F4 deferred: 'one pending at a time' forecloses 'show me 4, pick one' UX. v0.1.5 ticket S3-4 documents N-rejects-1-accept pattern with shared batch_id; first-class API in v0.3.0/S5 if CLI surfaces it.
tickets.notes: End-to-end integration tests: full critique cycle (reject -> reject -> refine -> accept), session persist + resume across YAML round-trip, human-readable YAML output verification.

deferred_findings: v0.1.5 / S3-1: GenerationRequest/Result types + generic Adapter[T]/Generator[T] (architect F1)
v0.1.5 / S3-2: Adapter stage/commit/discard split for deferred persistence (architect F3, F9)
v0.1.5 / S3-3: Determinism tier negotiation in Loop (architect F5)
v0.1.5 / S3-4: Document batch-propose pattern (architect F4)
v0.2.0 / S4-2: Persist-failure recovery surfacing raw_output (code F6 — surfaces with real generators)
v0.2.0 / S4-3: Single-int seed -> seed-as-extra-params convention (architect F11)
v0.2.0 / S4-4: cdd.testing protocol-conformance harness + InMemoryAdapter (architect F7)
v0.4.0 / S6-3: JSONL append mode for large logs (architect F8)
v0.6.0 / S8-4: Adapter.params_schema for replay validation (architect F10)
v0.7.0+ / S9-2: supports_seed -> honors_seed rename in API freeze (architect F14)
v0.1.5 / S3 incidental: SemVer parser tightening to reject negative ints, leading zeros, whitespace-padded values (R2 code N1)
v1.0.0 / S10-3: Subordinate FATHOMS in README (architect F16)

notable_findings: MAJOR (architect F1): Generator(prompt:str)->Any does not survive multi-modal/streaming/async pressure. Deferred to v0.1.5/S3-1 — the new sprint inserted specifically to fold protocol-shape findings before v0.2.0 calcifies the wrong shape.

MAJOR (architect F2): Schema version was int 1 with strict-equality. Fix-folded to SemVer 'MAJOR.MINOR' string with major-equal + minor-tolerant parsing. Forward-compat warning via warnings.warn(stacklevel=3).
MAJOR (architect F12): No multi-session authorship lineage. Fix-folded by adding Log.parent_session_id field. Cheap to add now; impossible to retrofit post-v1.0 without breaking replay tools.
MAJOR (code F1): read_log on empty/null YAML crashed with AttributeError. Fix-folded; 3 negative tests added.
MAJOR (code F2): from_dict bypassed Log.append chain validation. Fix-folded by replaying every entry through append; 2 negative tests added.

MAJOR (code F3): Path traversal in TextFileAdapter via crafted namespace or output_ref. Fix-folded with _resolve_within_base + _validate_namespace; 4 negative tests added including absolute-path injection.

Roadmap restructured: 9 sprints to 10. New S3 'v0.1.5 — Protocol hardening' inserted between v0.1.0 and v0.2.0 specifically to fold deferred protocol-shape findings before any external consumer (Anthropic generator, CLI, FATHOMS adapter) calcifies the current shape.
